executive_summary: Build failure analysis for scib_demo repository on main branch (Attempt 3). Critical configuration error detected: GitHub Actions workflow file not found at expected path .github/workflows/build.yml. This prevents any build execution from occurring. The workflow dispatch API returned HTTP 404, indicating the workflow file is either missing, incorrectly named, or not accessible with current permissions.

errors.title: GitHub Actions Workflow File Not Found
errors.description: The GitHub Actions workflow dispatch endpoint returned HTTP 404 Not Found when attempting to trigger the build.yml workflow. This indicates the workflow file does not exist at the expected path or is not accessible.
errors.error_message: HTTP Error 404: Not Found. Workflow dispatch endpoint returned 404. Possible causes: token missing workflow/actions permissions, workflow not accessible on this repo/branch, or Actions disabled. dispatch_url=https://api.github.com/repos/loginkarun/scib_demo/actions/workflows/.github%2Fworkflows%2Fbuild.yml/dispatches response_body={"message":"Not Found","documentation_url":"https://docs.github.com/rest/actions/workflows#create-a-workflow-dispatch-event","status":"404"}
errors.root_cause: The workflow file .github/workflows/build.yml does not exist in the repository at the specified branch (main), or the GitHub token lacks sufficient permissions to access/dispatch the workflow, or GitHub Actions is disabled for this repository.
errors.impact: Complete build pipeline failure. No builds can be executed until the workflow file is created or permissions are corrected. This blocks all CI/CD automation including compilation, testing, and deployment processes.

errors.title: Workflow Dispatch API Authentication/Permission Issue
errors.description: The GitHub API token may be missing required workflow or actions permissions, preventing workflow dispatch operations.
errors.error_message: Possible causes: token missing workflow/actions permissions, workflow not accessible on this repo/branch, or Actions disabled.
errors.root_cause: The GitHub personal access token (PAT) used for API calls may not have the 'workflow' scope enabled, which is required to dispatch workflow events. Alternatively, the token may not have write access to the repository.
errors.impact: Unable to programmatically trigger builds via API. Manual workflow triggers may still work if the issue is token-specific, but automated CI/CD pipelines are blocked.

errors.title: GitHub Actions Potentially Disabled
errors.description: GitHub Actions may be disabled at the repository or organization level, preventing any workflow execution.
errors.root_cause: GitHub Actions feature may be disabled in repository settings, or organization policies may restrict Actions usage.
errors.impact: No CI/CD automation possible until Actions are enabled. All build, test, and deployment workflows are non-functional.

steps: 3. Add workflow configuration with workflow_dispatch trigger
4. Define build jobs with Maven/Gradle build steps
5. Commit and push to main branch
6. Verify workflow appears in Actions tab
recommended_fixes.automation_script: Use GitHub Feature Branch Script Committer to create .github/workflows/build.yml with standard Maven build configuration
recommended_fixes.validation: Verify workflow file exists via GitHub API: GET /repos/loginkarun/scib_demo/contents/.github/workflows/build.yml

steps: 1. Navigate to GitHub Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens
2. Locate the token used for API calls (token redacted for security)
3. Edit token and ensure 'workflow' scope is enabled
4. Ensure 'repo' scope includes full repository access
6. Update token in automation scripts/environment variables
recommended_fixes.validation: Test workflow dispatch API call returns 204 No Content (success) instead of 404

steps: 1. Navigate to repository Settings > Actions > General
2. Under 'Actions permissions', select 'Allow all actions and reusable workflows'
3. Ensure 'Allow GitHub Actions to create and approve pull requests' is enabled if needed
4. Check organization-level Actions policies if applicable
recommended_fixes.automation_script: Requires repository admin access - manual configuration
recommended_fixes.validation: Verify Actions tab is accessible and workflows can be viewed

compliance_notes: Configuration errors require manual intervention and cannot be fully automated
Token permission changes require security review and approval
Workflow file creation should follow organization's CI/CD standards and templates
All changes should be tracked in audit logs for compliance

audit_trail.analysis_method: Automated log parsing and error classification
audit_trail.reason_for_manual_review: Configuration errors require repository admin access to resolve
